examples.title: String Concatenation with + Operator
examples.description: Demonstrates multiple concatenations creating new String objects
examples.explanation: Each '+=' operation creates a new String object due to immutability. In loops, this leads to O(n²) time complexity.

examples.title: Algorithm: String Concatenation Process
examples.description: Steps involved in concatenating multiple strings using + operator
examples.code:
ALGORITHM ConcatenateStrings
BEGIN
 INPUT strings s1, s2
 CREATE new character array of length s1.length + s2.length
 COPY s1 characters to new array
 COPY s2 characters to new array
 OUTPUT new string from combined array
END
examples.explanation: Each concatenation operation requires creating a new array and copying both strings. Repeated concatenations multiply this cost.
examples.timeComplexity: O(k*m) where k=number of strings, m=average length

examples.title: String Pool and Interning
examples.description: Demonstrates reference comparison and string interning
examples.explanation: == compares object references. intern() adds string to pool or returns existing reference. equals() compares content.

examples.title: Data Conversion with valueOf()
examples.description: Converting various data types to String using valueOf()
examples.explanation: valueOf() safely converts primitives and objects to strings. Handles null without exceptions.

examples.title: Custom toString() Implementation
examples.description: Overriding toString() for meaningful object representation
examples.explanation: Default toString() outputs class@hashcode. Overriding provides human-readable format for debugging.
